name: poznote
description: Poznote is a note-taking and documentation platform.
readme_summary: Poznote is a self-hosted, open-source note-taking and documentation platform built with JavaScript. It allows users to create and manage notes, tasks, and wikis, with features like Git synchronization, backup/export, and offline viewing.
primary_use_case: A self-hosted platform for personal or team note-taking, documentation, and task management.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, open book on a wooden desk, with glowing digital pages unfurling to reveal organized notes, to-do lists, and interconnected wiki-like diagrams. A subtle Git commit icon hovers near the book, and a small, clean UI element resembling a web interface is visible on a nearby tablet, displaying the Poznote logo. The overall mood is organized and productive, with soft, ambient lighting. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic, emphasizing clarity and accessibility.

name: colota
readme_summary: Colota is a self-hosted GPS location tracking application for Android devices. It prioritizes user privacy by sending location data directly to a user-controlled server without any analytics or telemetry, and offers robust offline functionality.
key_features: Multiple authentication methods (Basic Auth, Token, mTLS)
primary_use_case: Enabling users to track their location privately and securely by sending data to their own server, with comprehensive offline capabilities.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, antique compass with a glowing digital needle, pointing resolutely towards a small, personalized server icon in the distance. The compass face is subtly overlaid with a faint, abstract map texture, and digital data streams flow like wind around it. In the background, a silhouette of an Android phone is visible, emitting a gentle beam of light towards the compass. The overall mood is one of secure, private control over personal data. The scene should be rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, modern aesthetic and a color palette of deep blues, greens, and subtle metallic accents, emphasizing privacy and reliability. The repository name 'Colota' is subtly etched onto the compass's base.

name: forge
description: A Python framework for self-hosted LLM tool-calling and multi-step agentic workflows
readme_summary: Forge is a Python framework designed to enhance the reliability of self-hosted Large Language Model (LLM) tool-calling and agentic workflows. It acts as a reliability layer, applying guardrails such as response validation, parsing rescue, and retry nudges to LLM interactions, even without predefined workflow structures. Forge can be used as a proxy server, a workflow runner, or as middleware within existing orchestration loops, supporting various local and cloud-based LLM backends.
key_features: Reliability layer for self-hosted LLM tool-calling
Guardrails: rescue parsing, retry nudges, response validation
Opt-in workflow structure: required_steps, prerequisites, terminal_tool
Proxy server mode (OpenAI/Anthropic APIs) for transparent guardrails
WorkflowRunner for structured agent loops with SlotWorker for shared inference slots
Guardrails middleware for integration into custom orchestration loops
Supports Ollama, llama-server (llama.cpp), Llamafile, vLLM, and Anthropic backends
primary_use_case: Enhancing the reliability and robustness of tool-calling and agentic workflows for self-hosted Large Language Models.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan meticulously assembling intricate clockwork mechanisms, where each gear and spring represents a specific tool or function. The artisan is carefully guiding a glowing thread of data from a large, abstract 'LLM Brain' towards the clockwork. Some gears are already spinning smoothly, indicating successful tool calls, while others are being gently nudged or corrected by the artisan's tools, symbolizing forge's guardrails. The background is a clean, modern workshop with subtle digital interfaces displaying logs and success rates. The overall mood is one of precision, reliability, and controlled automation. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, slightly futuristic aesthetic and a balanced color palette.

name: streambert
description: A cross-platform Electron Desktop App to stream and download any Movie, TV Series or Anime in the World. Zero Ads and Tracking
readme_summary: Streambert is a cross-platform Electron desktop application designed for streaming and downloading movies, TV series, and anime. It offers a user-friendly interface with features like subtitle management, library tracking, and customization, while ensuring a completely ad-free and tracker-free experience.
key_features: Stream Movies, TV Series, and Anime
Download Movies, TV Series, and Anime
Library for tracking watched and saved content
primary_use_case: To provide a unified, ad-free desktop application for users to stream and download a wide variety of video content, including movies, TV series, and anime, from various online sources.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, stylized illustration of a user's desktop, where a sleek, modern application window (labeled 'Streambert') acts as a portal. From this portal, diverse content streams like movie reels, TV show icons, and anime characters flow outwards, being neatly organized into a digital library on one side and downloaded into organized folders on the other. Subtle glowing lines connect the portal to various content sources, representing seamless streaming and downloading. The overall mood is one of effortless access and organized entertainment, with a clean, futuristic aesthetic. The art style should be 3D isometric illustration with bright, inviting colors and clear iconography.
